rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope only when binding implementation-level details; use component type scope for abstract input/output contracts.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architectural boundaries: component type annexes must use only publicly visible features, not subcomponent paths.
Implementation-level annexes may bind subcomponent ports only after those subcomponents and connections are declared.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use the syntax: annex agree {** ... **};
Boolean expressions in guarantees must match the data type of the referenced port (e.g., bool, int, real).
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not use subcomponent port names in component type annexes unless exposed through visible features.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
type scope range assumption and guarantee
